layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: regressions and allows the reader to evaluate the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: variance explained-parsimony trade-off.

layout_dets.text: SN1:

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: People who influence my behavior think that I should use the system.

layout_dets.text: SN2:

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: People who are important to me think that I should use the system.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The senior management of this business has been helpful in the use of the system.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: SF4:

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: In general, the organization has supported the use of the system.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Performance expectancy

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: I would find the system useful in my job.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: RA1:

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Using the system enables me to accomplish tasks more quickly.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: RA5:

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Using the system increases my productivity.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: OE7:

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: If I use the system, I will increase my chances of getting a raise.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Effort expectancy

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: EOU3: My interaction with the system would be clear and understandable.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: EOU5:

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: It would be easy for me to become skillful at using the system.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: EOU6:

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: I would find the system easy to use.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: EU4:

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Learning to operate the system is easy for me.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Facilitating conditions

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: PBC2:

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: I have the resources necessary to use the system.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: PBC3:

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: I have the knowledge necessary to use the system.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: PBC5:

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The system is not compatible with other systems I use.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: FC3:

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: A specific person (or group) is available for assistance with system difficulties.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: SE4:

layout_dets.text: If I could call someone for help if I got stuck.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: SE6:

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: If I had a lot of time to complete the job for which the software was provided.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: SE7:

layout_dets.text: If I had just the built-in help facility for assistance.

layout_dets.text: Cross-Validation of UTAUT

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Data were gathered from two additional organi-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: zations to further validate UTAUT and add

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: external validity to the preliminary test. The major

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: details regarding the two participating organi-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: zations are provided in Table 18. The data were

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: collected on the same timeline as studies 1 and 2

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (see Figure 2). The data analysis procedures

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: were the same as the previous studies. The

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: results were consistent with studies 1 and 2. The

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: items used in the preliminary test of UTAUT (listed

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: in Table 16) were used to estimate the mea-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: surement and structural models in the new data.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: This helped ensure the test of the same model in

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: both the preliminary test and this validation, thus

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: limiting any variation due to the changing of items.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: UTAUT was then tested separately for each time

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: points of measurement by converting time period

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (experience) into a moderator. The column titled

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: "Pooled Analysis" reports the results of this

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: analysis. Caution is necessary when conducting

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: such analyses and the reader is referred to

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Appendix A for a discussion of the potential con-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: straints of pooling. Also reported in Appendix A

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: are the statistical tests that we conducted prior to

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: pooling the data for the analysis in Table 17.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: nonsignificant determinants (i.e., self-efficacy,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: anxiety, and attitude) were dropped from the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: model and the model was reestimated; facilitating

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: conditions was not dropped from the model

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: because of its role in predicting use.14 The re-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: estimated model results are shown in Table 17.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: relationships, the supportive results should be inter-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: preted with caution after consideration of the power

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: analyses reported in the text. The results are presented

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: here for model completeness and to allow the reader to

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: link the current research with existing theoretical

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: perspectives in this domain.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: relationships, the supportive results should be inter-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: preted with caution, bearing in mind the power analysis

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: reported in the text. In this case, the concern about the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: lack of a relationship is somewhat alleviated as some

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: previous research in this area has found attitude and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: facilitating conditions to not be predictors of intention in

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the presence of performance expectancy and effort

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: expectancy constructs (see Taylor and Todd 1995b;

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Venkatesh 2000).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: MIS Quarterly Vol. 27 No. 3/September 2003

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: In predicting usage behavior (Table 17b), both

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: behavioral intention (H6) and facilitating conditions

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: were significant, with the latter's effect being

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: moderated by age (the effect being more impor-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tant to older workers); and, based on Chow's test

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of the beta differences (p < .05), the effect was

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: stronger with increasing experience with the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: technology (H4b). Since H4a, H5a, H5b, and H5c

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: are hypothesized such that the predictor is not

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: expected to have an effect on the dependent

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: variable, a power analysis was conducted to

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: examine the potential for type II error. The likeli-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: hood of detecting medium effects was over 95

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: percent for an alpha level of .05 and the likelihood

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of detecting small effects was under 50 pecent.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: As expected, the effect of performance expec-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tancy was in the form of a three-way inter-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: action—the effect was moderated by gender and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: age such that it was more salient to younger

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: workers, particularly men, thus supporting H1.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Note that a direct effect for performance expec-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tancy on intention was observed; however, these

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: main effects are not interpretable due to the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: presence of interaction terms (e.g., Aiken and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: West 1991). The effect of effort expectancy was

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: via a three-way interaction—the effect was moder-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ated by gender and age (more salient to women

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and more so to older women). Based on Chow's

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: test of beta differences (p < .05), effort expectancy

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: was more significant with limited exposure to the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: technology (effect decreasing with experience),

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: thus supporting H2. The effect of social influence

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: was via a four-way interaction—with its role being

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: more important in the context of mandatory use,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: more so among women, and even more so among

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: older women. The Chow's test of beta differences

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (p < .05) indicated that social influence was even

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: more significant in the early stages of individual

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: experience with the technology, thus supporting

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: H3. Facilitating conditions was nonsignificant as

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: a determinant of intention, thus supporting H4a.13

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: As expected self-efficacy, anxiety, and attitude

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: did not have any direct effect on intention, thus

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: supporting H5a, H5b, and H5c. Three of the four

layout_dets.text: Discussion

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Thus, UTAUT is a definitive model that synthe-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: sizes what is known and provides a foundation to

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: guide future research in this area. By encom-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: passing the combined explanatory power of the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: individual models and key moderating influences,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: UTAUT advances cumulative theory while re-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: taining a parsimonious structure. Figure 3 pre-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: sents the model proposed and supported.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Table 22 presents a summary of the findings. It

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: should be noted that performance expectancy

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: appears to be a determinant of intention in most

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: situations: the strength of the relationship varies

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: with gender and age such that it is more signi-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ficant for men and younger workers. The effect of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: effort expectancy on intention is also moderated

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: by gender and age such that it is more significant

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: for women and older workers, and those effects

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: decrease with experience. The effect of social

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: influence on intention is contingent on all four

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: moderators included here such that we found it to

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: be nonsignificant when the data were analyzed

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: without the inclusion of moderators. Finally, the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: effect of facilitating conditions on usage was only

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: significant when examined in conjunction with the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: moderating effects of age and experience—i.e.,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: they only matter for older workers in later stages

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of experience.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: period (N = 133 at each time period). The mea-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: surement models are shown in Tables 19 and 20.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The pattern of results in this validation (Tables

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: 21(a) and 21(b)) mirrors what was found in the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: preliminary test (Table 17). The last column of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Tables 21(a) and 21(b) reports observations from

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the pooled analysis as before. Appendix B reports

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the statistical tests we conducted prior to pooling

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the data for the cross-validation test, consistent

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: with the approach taken in the preliminary test.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Insofar as the no-relationship hypotheses were

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: concerned, the power analysis revealed a high

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: likelihood (over 95 percent) of detecting medium

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: effects. The variance explained was quite com-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: parable to that found in the preliminary test of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: UTAUT.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: over any of the original eight models and their

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: extensions. Further, UTAUT was successful in

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: integrating key elements from among the initial set

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of 32 main effects and four moderators as

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: determinants of intention and behavior collectively

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: posited by eight alternate models into a model that

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: incorporated four main effects and four

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: moderators.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: MIS Quarterly Vol. 27 No. 3/September 2003

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The present research set out to integrate the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: fragmented theory and research on individual

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: acceptance of information technology into a uni-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: fied theoretical model that captures the essential

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: elements of eight previously established models.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: First, we identified and discussed the eight

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: specific models of the determinants of intention

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and usage of information technology. Second,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: these models were empirically compared using

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: within-subjects, longitudinal data from four organi-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: zations. Third, conceptual and empirical simi-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: larities across the eight models were used to

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: formulate the Unified Theory of Acceptance and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Use of Technology (UTAUT). Fourth, the UTAUT

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: was empirically tested using the original data from

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the four organizations and then cross-validated

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: using new data from an additional two organi-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: zations. These tests provided strong empirical

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: support for UTAUT, which posits three direct

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: determinants of intention to use (performance

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: expectancy, effort expectancy, and social influ-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ence) and two direct determinants of usage

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: behavior (intention and facilitating conditions).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Significant moderating influences of experience,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: voluntariness, gender, and age were confirmed as

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: integral features of UTAUT. UTAUT was able to

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: account for 70 percent of the variance (adjusted

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: R²) in usage intention—a substantial improvement

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Prior to discussing the implications of this work, it

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: is necessary to recognize some of its limitations.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: One limitation concerns the scales used to mea-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: sure the core constructs. For practical analytical

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: reasons, we operationalized each of the core

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: constructs in UTAUT by using the highest-loading

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: items from each of the respective scales. This

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: approach is consistent with recommendations in

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the psychometric literature (e.g., Nunnally and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Bernstein 1994). Such pruning of the instrument

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: was the only way to have the degrees of freedom

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: necessary to model the various interaction terms

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: From a theoretical perspective, UTAUT provides

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: a refined view of how the determinants of intention

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and behavior evolve over time. It is important to

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: for each of the constructs with an emphasis on

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: content validity, and then revalidating the model

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: specified herein (or extending it accordingly) with

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the new measures. Our research employed stan-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: dard measures of intention, but future research

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: should examine alternative measures of intention

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and behavior in revalidating or extending the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: research presented here to other contexts.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: MIS Quarterly Vol. 27 No. 3/September 2003

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: at the item level as recommended by Chin et al.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (1996). However, one danger of this approach is

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: that facets of each construct can be eliminated,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: thus threatening content validity. Specifically, we

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: found that choosing the highest-loading items

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: resulted in items from some of the models not

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: being represented in some of the core constructs

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (e.g., items from MPCU were not represented in

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: performance expectancy). Therefore, the mea-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: sures for UTAUT should be viewed as preliminary

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and future research should be targeted at more

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: fully developing and validating appropriate scales

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: emphasize that most of the key relationships in

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the model are moderated. For example, age has

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: received very little attention in the technology

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: acceptance research literature, yet our results

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: indicate that it moderates all of the key relation-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ships in the model. Gender, which has received

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: some recent attention, is also a key moderating

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: influence; however, consistent with findings in the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: sociology and social psychology literature (e.g.,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Levy 1988), it appears to work in concert with age,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: a heretofore unexamined interaction. For exam-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ple, prior research has suggested that effort

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: expectancy is more salient for women (e.g.,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Venkatesh and Morris 2000). While this may be

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: true, our findings suggest this is particularly true

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: for the older generation of workers and those with

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: relatively little experience with a system. While

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: existing studies have contributed to our under-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: standing of gender and age influences indepen-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: dently, the present research illuminates the inter-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: play of these two key demographic variables and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: adds richness to our current understanding of the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: phenomenon. We interpret our findings to sug-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: gest that as the younger cohort of employees in

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the workforce mature, gender differences in how

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: each perceives information technology may

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: disappear. This is a hopeful sign and suggests

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: that oft-mentioned gender differences in the use of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: information technology may be transitory, at least

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: as they relate to a younger generation of workers

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: raised and educated in the Digital Age.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The complex nature of the interactions observed,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: particularly for gender and age, raises several

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: interesting issues to investigate in future research,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: especially given the interest in today's societal

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and workplace environments to create equitable

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: settings for women and men of all ages. Future

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: research should focus on identifying the potential

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: "magic number" for age where effects begin to

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: appear (say for effort expectancy) or disappear

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (say for performance expectancy). While gender

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and age are the variables that reveal an inter-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: esting pattern of results, future research should

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: identify the underlying influential mechanisms—

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: potential candidates here include computer

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: literacy and social or cultural background, among

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: others. Finally, although gender moderates three

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: key relationships, it is imperative to understand

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the importance of gender roles and the possibility

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: that "psychological gender" is the root cause for

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the effects observed. Empirical evidence has

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: demonstrated that gender roles can have a pro-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: found impact on individual attitudes and behaviors

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: both within and outside the workplace (e.g., Baril

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: et al. 1989; Feldman and Aschenbrenner 1983;

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Jagacinski 1987; Keys 1985; Roberts 1997; Sachs

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: et al. 1992; Wong et al. 1985). Specifically, gen-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: der effects observed here could be a manifesta-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tion of effects caused by masculinity, femininity,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and androgyny rather than just "biological sex"

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (e.g., Lubinski et al. 1983). Future work might be

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: directed at more closely examining the importance

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of gender roles and exploring the socio-psycho-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: logical basis for gender as a means for better

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: understanding its moderating role.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: MIS Quarterly Vol. 27 No. 3/September 2003

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: As is evident from the literature, the role of social

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: influence constructs has been controversial.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Some have argued for their inclusion in models of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: adoption and use (e.g., Taylor and Todd 1995b;

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Thompson et al. 1991), while others have not

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: included them (e.g., Davis et al. 1989). Previous

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: work has found social influence to be significant

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: only in mandatory settings (see Hartwick and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Barki 1994; Venkatesh and Davis 2000). Other

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: work has found social influence to be more

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: significant among women in early stages of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: experience (e.g., Venkatesh and Morris 2000).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Still other research has found social influence to

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: be more significant among older workers (e.g.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Morris and Venkatesh 2000). This research is

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: among the first to examine these moderating influ-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ences in concert. Our results suggest that social

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: influences do matter; however, they are more

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: likely to be salient to older workers, particularly

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: women, and even then during early stages of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: experience/adoption. This pattern mirrors that for

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: effort expectancy with the added caveat that social

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: influences are more likely to be important in

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: mandatory usage settings. The contingencies

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: identified here provide some insights into the way

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: in which social influences change over time and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: may help explain some of the equivocal results

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: reported in the literature. By helping to clarify the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: contingent nature of social influences, this paper

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: sheds light on when social influence is likely to

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: play an important role in driving behavior and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: when it is less likely to do so.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Venkatesh et al./User Acceptance of IT

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: UTAUT underscores this point and highlights the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: importance of contextual analysis in developing

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: strategies for technology implementation within

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: organizations. While each of the existing models

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: in the domain is quite successful in predicting

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: technology usage behavior, it is only when one

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: considers the complex range of potential moder-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ating influences that a more complete picture of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the dynamic nature of individual perceptions about

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: technology begins to emerge. Despite the ability

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of the existing models to predict intention and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: usage, current theoretical perspectives on indivi-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: dual acceptance are notably weak in providing

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: prescriptive guidance to designers. For example,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: applying any of the models presented here might

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: inform a designer that some set of individuals

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: might find a new system difficult to use. Future

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: research should focus on integrating UTAUT with

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: research that has identified causal antecedents of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the constructs used within the model (e.g.,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Karahanna and Straub 1999; Venkatesh 2000;

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Venkatesh and Davis 2000) in order to provide a

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: greater understanding of how the cognitive pheno-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: mena that were the focus of this research are

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: formed. Examples of previously examined deter-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: minants of the core predictors include system

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: characteristics (Davis et al. 1989) and self-efficacy

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (Venkatesh 2000). Additional determinants that

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: have not been explicitly tied into this stream but

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: merit consideration in future work include task-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: technology fit (Goodhue and Thompson 1994) and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: individual ability constructs such as "g"—general

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: cognitive ability/intelligence (Colquitt et al. 2000).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: While the variance explained by UTAUT is quite

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: high for behavioral research, further work should

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: attempt to identify and test additional boundary

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: conditions of the model in an attempt to provide

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: an even richer understanding of technology adop-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tion and usage behavior. This might take the form

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: of additional theoretically motivated moderating

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: influences, different technologies (e.g., collabora-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tive systems, e-commerce applications), different

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: user groups (e.g., individuals in different functional

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: areas), and other organizational contexts (e.g.,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: public or government institutions). Results from

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: such studies will have the important benefit of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: enhancing the overall generalizability of UTAUT

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and/or extending the existing work to account for

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: additional variance in behavior. Specifically, given

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: MIS Quarterly Vol. 27 No. 3/September 2003

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: the extensive moderating influences examined

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: here, a research study that examines the general-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: izability of these findings with significant repre-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: sentation in each cell (total number of cells: 24;

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: two levels of voluntariness, three levels of experi-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ence [no, limited, more], two levels of gender, and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: at least two levels of age [young vs. old]) would be

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: valuable. Such a study would allow a pairwise,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: inter-cell comparison using the rigorous Chow's

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: test and provide a clear understanding of the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: nature of effects for each construct in each cell.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Related to the predictive validity of this class of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: models in general and UTAUT in particular is the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: role of intention as a key criterion in user accep-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tance research—future research should investi-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: gate other potential constructs such as behavioral

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: expectation (Warshaw and Davis 1985) or habit

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (Venkatesh et al. 2000) in the nomological net-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: work. Employing behavioral expectation will help

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: account for anticipated changes in intention

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: (Warshaw and Davis 1985) and thus shed light

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: even in the early stages of the behavior about the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: actual likelihood of behavioral performance since

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: intention only captures internal motivations to

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: perform the behavior. Recent evidence suggests

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: that sustained usage behavior may not be the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: result of deliberated cognitions and are simply

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: routinized or automatic responses to stimuli (see

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: Venkatesh et al. 2000).

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: One of the most important directions for future

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: research is to tie this mature stream of research

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: into other established streams of work. For

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: example, little to no research has addressed the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: link between user acceptance and individual or

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: organizational usage outcomes. Thus, while it is

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: often assumed that usage will result in positive

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: outcomes, this remains to be tested. The unified

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: model presented here might inform further inquiry

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: into the short- and long-term effects of information

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: technology implementation on job-related out-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: comes such as productivity, job satisfaction,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: organizational commitment, and other perfor-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: mance-oriented constructs. Future research

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: should study the degree to which systems per-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: ceived as successful from an IT adoption per-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: spective (i.e., those that are liked and highly used

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: by users) are considered a success from an

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: organizational perspective.

layout_dets.text: Following from the Unified Theory of Acceptance

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and Use of Technology presented here, future

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: research should focus on identifying constructs

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: that can add to the prediction of intention and

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: behavior over and above what is already known

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and understood. Given that UTAUT explains as

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: much as 70 percent of the variance in intention, it

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: is possible that we may be approaching the

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: practical limits of our ability to explain individual

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: acceptance and usage decisions in organizations.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: In the study of information technology implemen-

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: tations in organizations, there has been a

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: proliferation of competing explanatory models of

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: individual acceptance of information technology.

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: The present work advances individual acceptance

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: research by unifying the theoretical perspectives

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: common in the literature and incorporating four

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: moderators to account for dynamic influences

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: including organizational context, user experience,

layout_dets.label: ocr_text
layout_dets.text: and demographic characteristics.
